heading: about

blocks.title: about
blocks.body:
# `Biography`

`Arash Famiilirani is a Montreal-based electroacoustic composer and sound artist whose work`

`explores the boundaries between perception, time, and minimal sonic materials. A graduate of`

`Concordia University’s Bachelor of Fine Arts program in Electroacoustic Studies, Famiilirani’s`

`practice draws from both experimental composition and attentive listening, transforming subtle`

`gestures and everyday sounds into immersive auditory experiences. His recent works`

`investigate psychoacoustic phenomena and the tension between structure and stillness,`

`inviting audiences to engage with sound as a living, perceptual field.`

## `Education`

`Bachelor of Fine Arts 2016-2021`

`Electroacoustic`

`Concordia University, Montreal, Quebec`

`Diploma in Music 2003-2007`

`Classical Theory and performance`

`Tehran Conservatoire (Nahavand Higher Education Institute), Tehran, Iran`

### `Art and Music`

`Sound Engineer, Concordia Chamber Jazz Ensemble Oscar Peterson Concert Hall Montreal,`

`Canada, 2022`

`Sound Engineer, Concordia Choir and Chamber Choir Oscar Peterson Concert Hall Montreal,`

`Canada, 2021`

`Composer, 60x60 Concordia Montreal, Canada, 2020`

`Composer, 60x60 Concordia Montreal, Canada, 2019`

`Live Sound Designer/Performer, CLOrk goes Free Jazz, with Michael Pinsonneault and Eden`

`Bahar, Concordia University Montreal, Canada, 2019`

`Composer, 60x60 Concordia Montreal, Canada, 2018`

`Producing Station IDs for radio CJLO 1690 Montreal, Canada, 2018`

`Live Sound Designer/Performer, Concordia Laptop Orchestra with Andrea Young, Concordia`

`University Montreal, Canada, 2018Live Sound Designer/Performer, Set Phasers To Stun: CLOrk, Amanda Christie, & Damaris Baker,`

`Eastern Bloc, Montreal, Canada, 2017`

`Composer and Sound Artist, Darchin Duo Tehran, Iran, 2007 - 2015`

`Performer, Jazz Quintet, Da House, Tehran, Iran, 2014`

`Performer, Jazz Trio, Shamlou, Tehran, Iran, 2014`

`Performer, Jazz Quartet Orf, Tehran, Iran, 2012`

`Performer, Tehran conservatoir choire at Tehran University Tehran, Iran, 2011`

modules.title: bio
modules.body:
Biography

Arash Famiilirani is a Montreal-based electroacoustic composer and sound artist whose work

explores the boundaries between perception, time, and minimal sonic materials. A graduate of

Concordia University’s Bachelor of Fine Arts program in Electroacoustic Studies, Famiilirani’s

practice draws from both experimental composition and attentive listening, transforming subtle

gestures and everyday sounds into immersive auditory experiences. His recent works

investigate psychoacoustic phenomena and the tension between structure and stillness,

inviting audiences to engage with sound as a living, perceptual field.

bio:
# Biography

Arash Famiilirani is a Montreal-based electroacoustic composer and sound artist whose work

explores the boundaries between perception, time, and minimal sonic materials. A graduate of

Concordia University’s Bachelor of Fine Arts program in Electroacoustic Studies, Famiilirani’s

practice draws from both experimental composition and attentive listening, transforming subtle

gestures and everyday sounds into immersive auditory experiences. His recent works

investigate psychoacoustic phenomena and the tension between structure and stillness,

inviting audiences to engage with sound as a living, perceptual field.

## Education

Bachelor of Fine Arts 2016-2021

Electroacoustic

name: Arash Famiilirani

sections.title: about
sections.body:
Biography

Arash Famiilirani is a Montreal-based electroacoustic composer and sound artist whose work

explores the boundaries between perception, time, and minimal sonic materials. A graduate of

Concordia University’s Bachelor of Fine Arts program in Electroacoustic Studies, Famiilirani’s

practice draws from both experimental composition and attentive listening, transforming subtle

gestures and everyday sounds into immersive auditory experiences. His recent works

investigate psychoacoustic phenomena and the tension between structure and stillness,

inviting audiences to engage with sound as a living, perceptual field.
